entry.title: YARGAllSetlists v26.4.4
entry.content:
This release only contains a bugfix that only sometimes caused generation failures with the All Setlists apworld if 2 or more copies of the same song appeared in a seed. This can happen due to the same song appearing in multiple setlists.
This bugfix does not prevent duplicate songs from appearing in seeds but rather properly handles it.
Only the All Setlists world was changed. No other worlds were affected by this bug.

entry.title: Buckshot Roulette v0.3.0
entry.content:
## Features - **Consumable Item Randomization** - Item unlocks are shuffled randomly - **Steam Achievements** - Obtain items from completing achievements - **Shotsanity** - Obtain items from successful shots - **Streaksanity** - Obtain items from successful shots on the dealer in a row without being shot yourself - **Customizable Goal** - Choose from one of the following goals: - **70K**: Beat the game - **140K**: Beat 6 Double or Nothing rounds in a row - **1000K**: Cash out in Double or Nothing mode with a score of at least 1 million - **Custom**: Cash out in Double or Nothing mode with a user-specified minimum score - **Consumable Item Logic** - Customize how many consumable are expected to beat a certain number of rounds - **Custom Mechanics** - Unique gameplay features specific to the Archipelago world - **Hint Mode** - Complete rounds and achievements to get hints for locations in other slots in an Archipelago game ## Changelog ### World #### Item Buffs and Debuffs - **Item Buffs**: Finding "Item Buff" items in the multiworld will apply a permanent buff to your consumable items. - **Item Debuffs**: Start with a selection of consumable item debuffed. Finding an item's respective "Clear Debuff" in the multiworld will remove its debuff.
| Item | Buff Description | Debuff Description | |------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Hand Saw | Deals an extra point of damage (2 → 3) | 25% chance to also deal 1 damage to the player *Not implemented* | | Magnifying Glass | Gains the ability to convert the current shell to either live or blank | 40% chance to gain no information when viewing the current shell | | Beer | Gains the ability to clear the shotgun, immediately moving on to the next batch | Adds 15 to the poison gauge *(see below)* | | Cigarette Pack | 50% chance to heal 2 charges instead of 1 | Adds 30 to the poison gauge *(see below)* | | Handcuffs | Dealer skips one extra turn (1 → 2) | 25% chance for the dealer to immediately break free | | Expired Medicine | Buffs chance of healing (1 in 2 → 5 in 6) | Failing the coin flip will also add 20 to the poison gauge *(see below)* | | Burner Phone | Gains the ability to select which shell to get info about (2nd - 7th) | 25% chance to get no information | | Adrenaline | Gains the ability to steal the dealer's adrenaline | Adds 50 to the poison gauge *(see below)* | | Inverter | Applies a Schrodinger's Bullet Trap to the dealer | 25% chance to not invert the current shell | #### Poison Gauge - Certain item debuffs will affect a new mechanic known as the poison gauge. At the beginning of every turn, you will take damage according to the value of your poison gauge as follows:
| Poison Gauge | Guaranteed Damage Every Turn | Chance of Losing One Extra Charge | |:------------:|:----------------------------:|:---------------------------------:| | p | d = p / 100 rounded down | p / 100 - d | #### Other - Updated item and location IDs for most items and locations - Therefore, this release is **NOT** backwards compatible with previous releases. ### Client #### Item Buffs and Debuffs - Implemented all item buffs and most item debuffs - Added a display for the poison gauge #### Hint Mode - Made Double or Nothing mode immediately available from the start when playing in hint mode #### Other - Added in-game notifications when sending items - Added a display for your current streak - Fixed a bug where game would attempt to spawn a null instance of an item when the player's grid is full - Crashes should be much less common as a result - Fixed a bug where shotsanity count would not sync among players playing on the same slot - Fixed a bug where selecting the **Free** setting for the **Double or Nothing Requirements** would lock Double or Nothing mode behind the vanilla requirements ## Guides - [Info](<https://github.com/asdfwyay/APBuckshot/blob/dev/worlds/buckshot/docs/en_Buckshot Roulette.md>) - [Setup & Updating](https://github.com/asdfwyay/APBuckshot/blob/dev/worlds/buckshot/docs/setup_en.md)

entry.title: League of Legends v0.2.1
entry.content: - Enabled Checks option: You can decide which checks to enable, no more ARAM mode or "everything", tune it however you want. - _ARAM mode option **removed, MAKE SURE TO REDO YOUR YAMLS** (check the enabled_checks option description)_ - Win Completes Champion option: Makes a game win trigger the remaining checks for the played champion. - Disable special treatment for support champions (they’ll have the “kill X champions” check instead of vision score). - Connector improvements: Displays remaining checks for each character, champion table is sortable by columns.

entry.title: Crypt of the NecroDancer v0.5.3
entry.content:
### Changes - Add Sing Spell item as a unique item to generation, make Hatsune Miku require it for `soft` and `hard` `character_unlocks` setting
Please see the [setup file](https://github.com/lastingParadox/Archipelago-CotND/blob/crypt-of-the-necrodancer/worlds/cotnd/docs/setup_en.md) for getting started.
Please inform me of any issues through the associated [Crypt of the NecroDancer channel in the Archipelago Discord](https://discord.com/channels/731205301247803413/1192775219740422176) or through [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/lastingParadox/Archipelago-CotND/issues).

entry.title: Crypt of the NecroDancer v0.5.3
entry.content:
### Changes - Add DeathLinkType setting, along with two options: Tempo and Marv - Tempo: On Deathlink, trigger a countdown for 24 beats. If you can't escape the level in that timeframe, you die - Marv: Spawns a Marv familiar for the rest of the level. If Marv dies, you'll take 20% of your max hp in damage every beat and will likely die. Marv familiars despawn on escaping the level. - Add weapon materials always being precollected when `include_materials` is false, make them never precollected when `include_materials` setting is true. - Add unique items never being precollected when `include_unique_items` is true, aside from starting character requirements when `character_unlocks` is set to `soft` or `hard`. - Fix running Archipelago through appimage causing the client to not be able to find the apworld (thanks [studkid](https://github.com/studkid) for finding this) - Add rudimentary test suite for generation - Fix Pylance type issues
Please see the [setup file](https://github.com/lastingParadox/Archipelago-CotND/blob/crypt-of-the-necrodancer/worlds/cotnd/docs/setup_en.md) for getting started.
Please inform me of any issues through the associated [Crypt of the NecroDancer channel in the Archipelago Discord](https://discord.com/channels/731205301247803413/1192775219740422176) or through [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/lastingParadox/Archipelago-CotND/issues).

entry.title: Dark Cloud 1 v0.4.4
entry.content:
# Dark Cloud 1 AP Rando v0.4.4
## Changes Temporarily replaced Alnet's Llama with a Garnet at generation to prevent the player from getting the fishing rod until a cleaner client side fix can be implemented. Small update to the client to support the change as well.
No change to the .pnach file

entry.title: SMW: Spicy Mycena Waffles v1.3.1
entry.content: Small bug fix ## Info * [Information](https://thelx5.github.io/smw/) * [Full Changelog](https://thelx5.github.io/smw/changelog/#1.3.1) ## Changes * Fixed Blue Yoshi requirements being met by only being able to find a red/green Yoshi

entry.title: ULTRAKILL v3.4.5
entry.content: - Fixed generation failing when choosing a secret mission as the goal with the option for secret exits to unlock by finding items. - Added a safety check to the save slot menu when Archipelago data fails to load.

entry.title: Refunct v1.0.0
entry.content:
# Version 1.0.0 of Refunct for Archipelago! 🥳 🎆
The first version I consider "feature-complete" so it deserves the version 1.0.0 name! More to come though 😃 All of this wouldn't have been possible without the help of Oberien and Luke Saward from the Refunct Community and Nielrenned and Nepcore from the Archipelago Discord! ❤️ Also big thanks to everyone in both discords for feedback and reactions!! And to 360chrism for spreading this game far and wide!
⚠️ You will need a new yaml :)
## Changes ### Gameplay - Buttons (and platforms below buttons) in the main mode are now also proper checks. (thanks @nielrenned) - The final cluster, `Cluster 31`, is now part of the main mode and has several platform checks and a button check. (thanks @nielrenned) - You can now select a button to be the victory condition. The option is now called `Goal` instead of `Final Platform`. - Added a new minigame: Refunct Mountain, in which you play the vanilla game with just jump, jump pads, and... DASH??? Press E to use the dash. _This minigame is off by default because you can't rebind the controls in-game (yet)._ ### Smaller gameplay changes - Pressing a button in the main game mode also automatically pressed the platform below it (because it can be hard to touch the platform). - Reworked how "unknown" goals work, especially with a button as a goal (it shows a yellow beam but only after you've found it). - Extra cubes in the main mode are now spawned directly after clicking new game, which reduces lag when starting the main game mode. - Seeker minigame locations work different now, first it was platform `#1`, `#2`, `#3` etc, now locations are called `Seeker Minigame: Platform 28-20` for example. - Added 1 more block to Climb Minigames so we know for sure 100m is reachable without jumping. - Some minor logic optimizations. - The `debug.bat` now prints the full error trace which might help with troubleshooting. ### UI - The menu top-left also has a background now which should make it much easier to read. - The UI now shows in the main game mode if you can collect cubes and extra cubes. It doesn't show this line if cubes are fully disabled. ### Apworld - The Climb Minigames are now separated into three minigames in the options. - Added `Clusters`, `Minigames` and `Abilities` item groups. - Increased the max minigame limit to 9. - Renamed "Final Platform" to "Goal Location". ### Misc changes - The log now shows if you are on the latest version. - Fixed the issue that the game would crash with the wrong login info. - Updated the README with more troubleshooting. (thanks @nepcore for all the people you've helped setting up the game) ### Map editor - Maybe in the future it will be possible to add custom levels as AP levels. Press CTRL+SHIFT+M to open the Map Editor mode (this already existed in the original mod). More info about custom level submissions for this project is pinned in the AP discord (I'm picky 😄).

entry.title: Rain World v1.5.2
entry.content:
# See [the setup page](https://github.com/alphappy/ArchipelagoRW/blob/v1.5.0/worlds/rain_world/docs/setup_en.md) for instructions. # This release is intended to be used with [v1.5.4 of the client](https://github.com/SaltiestSyrup/RWRandomizer/releases/tag/v1.5.4). # Scroll down to Assets to find the player YAML templates and the .apworld.
## Player YAML Options - New `use_watcher_passages` option that will enable passage tokens in the Watcher campaign. Utilizing this in-game will require an external mod. (285b16cfa30c5272bc3c1467835e14938ba2e62c) - Submerged Superstructure is now select-able as a starting region (0a3c05b42f4a68d3367e02ddad656b8ec688669f) - Added Fire Egg to Saint's expanded food quest (d105adeb9c1dccdc6d52070e7ac4035d5b336997)
## Generation Fixes - Meeting Five Pebbles no longer logically requires The Mark (edc012f627b782f1072c241371c8b0525ab6186f) - Meeting Looks to the Moon as Hunter now logically requires the green neuron (edc012f627b782f1072c241371c8b0525ab6186f) - Wanderer pips once again only count story regions as progress (7098e43da6775612ab47bd1f5156a4bcfefa647d) - Fix Universal Tracker logic failing to account for having Expedition Perks, as well as other issues (e14db699995a36e1c320995b25f12688ea57e36c) - World information has been updated to match the changes made in game version 1.11.7b (e426e2aed4a34d3eb5159ff1253c6489e37fbcc2)

entry.title: Sonic Adventure DX v1.2.1rc0.post0
entry.content:
## [v1.2.1 pre-release 3] - 2026-04-04
### Changed:
- Water Tank is now always drained regardless of the Egg Carrier's transformation state.
### Fixed:
- Fixed starting character option being ignored. - Fixed incorrect logic for emblem requirements around the Egg Carrier.

entry.title: Taskipelago v0.6
entry.content:
# Version 0.6 Major version update refactoring the apworld to use the new Rule Builder for cleaner code and maintainability, as well as some feature additions. Requires Archipelago version 0.6.7+ for generation.
## Features, Improvements, and Changes * Goal task can now be selected * Refactor apworld to use new Rule Builder API * Refactor apworld into several files to conform to standard practice better * QOL: empty rewards now automatically populate as filler items on YAML export * QOL: Item names actually reflect the reward names in the multiworld
## Bug fixes * Checklist no longer marks tasks completed just because their reward has been collected by the multiworld.

entry.title: Universal Tracker v0.2.27
entry.content:
## What's Changed * clean up commands when we aren't using them to not crash /help by @qwint in https://github.com/FarisTheAncient/Archipelago/pull/122 * Use the size from the `map_locations` when specified, otherwise fallback on map size. by @Jouramie in https://github.com/FarisTheAncient/Archipelago/pull/125 * Improve item creation error logging by @Ars-Ignis in https://github.com/FarisTheAncient/Archipelago/pull/127 * we broke nogui again by @qwint in https://github.com/FarisTheAncient/Archipelago/pull/129 * Document map integration missing features by @Jouramie in https://github.com/FarisTheAncient/Archipelago/pull/128 * Finally Document the powershell script from digiholic * fully rebuilt the rendering for get_logical_path, we now use rule builder for each entrance and give worlds the ability to override entrance rending with the new override `explain_path`
## New Contributors * @Jouramie made their first contribution in https://github.com/FarisTheAncient/Archipelago/pull/125 * @Ars-Ignis made their first contribution in https://github.com/FarisTheAncient/Archipelago/pull/127
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/FarisTheAncient/Archipelago/compare/Tracker_v0.2.26...Tracker_v0.2.27

entry.title: Taskipelago v0.7b0
entry.content:
# Version 0.7 Beta Support for boolean logic implemented, but needs heavy testing
## Features, Improvements, and Changes * Boolean logic now added! You can now enter strings like 1, 2 && (3, 4 || 5, 6) into any field that accepts a list of tasks or rewards (goal tasks included!)
hotfix: oops i messed up one of the goal checking variables, fixed now

entry.title: Everhood 2 v0.4.2
entry.content:
> [!IMPORTANT] > While many softlocks have been fixed. Some softlocks may still occur. Some areas were untested such as Green Route 3 and many Hilbert Hotel rooms. Please bring my attention to them in Discord if they occur.
> [!NOTE] > Minimum version of the APWorld is now 0.6.7.
## New Features: - Colorsanity (Display name Randomise Color Reflects now) now has a new modes - Off: Can Reflect All Colors - Single Soul Piece: Only need 1 Soul Piece to reflect a colour - Multiple Soul Pieces: Need Multiple Soul Pieces to reflect colours for any enemy tied to an Time Hub area. - APWorld now written with Rule Builder. Should also now **partially** support Universal Tracker now? Needs more work anyway. - Colours are now "X Soul Piece". To make them stand out more.
## Client Fixes - Fixed Main Screen Button layout. Settings are now selectable again. - Fixed Auto-unlocking Everhood 1 and Bird Island in Door Key mode. - Death Coin from completing Bird Island should now correctly collect. - Updated Multiclient.net to 6.7.1. (Now more stable in larger syncs/asyncs) - Door Key Off fixes: - Home Town door now correctly spawns after defeating dragon. - Everhood 1 door will now spawn after entering Everhood 1 once. - No longer can activate the final sequence without having completed Everhood 1. (Could teleport back after defeating Dmitri) - Softlock fixes: - Force open the East Gate in the Mining Base Tunnels if you have defeated Gorilla once. - Can now trigger the briefing cutscene to get to the castle again in the Veggie War. - No longer get stuck trying to start the final Mushroom Bureau cutscene if you did not complete either side. - Fixed loading into Tutorial Spaceship no longer allowing you to interact with Boatman.
## AP World fixes - Fixed Hilbert Hotel and VIP Ticket not requiring its keys logically in non-door key options. - Actually add Bird Island and Everhood keys. - Fixed multiple issues with additional hub 1 areas and act 2 settings, when not doing door keys. - Actually made Irvine Pocket Dimension reliant on finishing Mushroom Bureau when not doing door keys. - A number of minor logic errors that were caught in the rewrite.
# Installation ### Melonloader *Melonloader is required to play with this mod. However reinstalling it is not required on update.*
1. Download MelonLoader.Installer.exe from https://github.com/LavaGang/MelonLoader/releases/latest and run it. 2. Choose the automated tab, click the select button and browse to `Everhood 2.exe`. - You can find the folder in steam by finding the game in your library, right clicking it and choosing *Manage→Browse Local Files*. - If you click the bar at the top telling you your current folder, this will give you a path you can copy. If you paste that into the window popped up by **MelonLoader**, it will automatically go to the same folder. 3. The launcher will automatically default to the latest version. - If the latest version is not working: **Untick Latest and select 0.6.6.** 4. Click install. Once finished run the game once.
### Mod Install
1.Download `ArchipelagoEverhood.zip`. 2. Head to the Everhood folder and open up `/mods/`. 3. Extract the contents of the .zip file into mods. - Ensure that `ArchipelagoEverhood.dll` and `Archipelago.MultiClient.Net.dll` are under `/mods/` and not within a sub folder. 4. Run the game. If a new button appears on the start screen the mod was installed correctly.

entry.title: Everhood 2 v0.4.3
entry.content:
> [!IMPORTANT] > While many softlocks have been fixed. Some softlocks may still occur. Some areas were untested such as Green Route 3 and many Hilbert Hotel rooms. Please bring my attention to them in Discord if they occur.
> [!NOTE] > Minimum version of the APWorld is now 0.6.7.
See 0.4.2 patch notes for the main changes: https://github.com/DeamonHunter/ArchipelagoEverhood2/releases/tag/v0.4.2
## AP World fixes - Fixed creating locations during the rule step, which could cause minor generation issues.
# Installation ### Melonloader *Melonloader is required to play with this mod. However reinstalling it is not required on update.*
1. Download MelonLoader.Installer.exe from https://github.com/LavaGang/MelonLoader/releases/latest and run it. 2. Choose the automated tab, click the select button and browse to `Everhood 2.exe`. - You can find the folder in steam by finding the game in your library, right clicking it and choosing *Manage→Browse Local Files*. - If you click the bar at the top telling you your current folder, this will give you a path you can copy. If you paste that into the window popped up by **MelonLoader**, it will automatically go to the same folder. 3. The launcher will automatically default to the latest version. - If the latest version is not working: **Untick Latest and select 0.6.6.** 4. Click install. Once finished run the game once.
### Mod Install
1.Download `ArchipelagoEverhood.zip`. 2. Head to the Everhood folder and open up `/mods/`. 3. Extract the contents of the .zip file into mods. - Ensure that `ArchipelagoEverhood.dll` and `Archipelago.MultiClient.Net.dll` are under `/mods/` and not within a sub folder. 4. Run the game. If a new button appears on the start screen the mod was installed correctly.

entry.title: Manual_BlockTales_WolfBoi008 v4.2.3
entry.content: # Known Issues None for now... # Changelog - The Worm Item will now be changed to Useful instead of Filler when Fishsanity is disabled, greatly decreasing its chances of being deleted when too few Checks are available for all of the Items. (@tomtherockstar) - Renamed the Cheezburger on Second Floor Check to say that its on the Third Floor. (@tomtherockstar) - Updated the Cards found in the In The Cards Shop. (@tomtherockstar) - Removed an excess Check for the Ye Olde Inn Shop. (gabr1e1_l) - Removed an excess Check for the Blackrock Castle Ice Cream Shop. (gabr1e1_l) - Added some new Chatsanity Checks, which include the following: - Ashgabat in Blackrock Forge, (gabr1e1_l) - The two fallen Guards in the Blackrock Castle Lobby (after dropping the chandelier on them), (gabr1e1_l) - The Cow and Sheep in Meadows [3], (gabr1e1_l) - And the Cameraman in Rugged Rainforest [3]. - Moved the Big Fire Ant Check from Ant Hills [2] to Ant Hills [3]. # Other Notes - If there was a change you reported, feel free to tell me, and I'll give credit to you in the changelog. - If you have a GitHub, I can tag you, and then you can tell your friends you're a contributor to the Manual! - I'm considering unmerging Shopsanity and Cutscenesanity to be Options like they used to be, since I found a solution to the gen errors issue. Keep an eye out, in case I decide to go through with this.

entry.title: The Legend of Zelda - Oracle of Seasons v20.1.1
entry.content:
## 🧠 Logic - Fixed logic still requiring 10 bombs in numerous places
## 🛠️ Bugfixes - Fixed Natsu bridge showing up - Fixed some randomized puzzles - Fixed the game not able to be patched if too many items came from other worlds

entry.title: The Legend of Zelda - Oracle of Seasons v20.1.2
entry.content:
## 🧠 Logic - Fixed logic still requiring 10 bombs in numerous places
## 🛠️ Bugfixes - Fixed Natsu bridge showing up - Fixed some randomized puzzles - Fixed the game not able to be patched if too many items came from other worlds

entry.title: Silver Daze v0.7.3
entry.content: Small update! It's tested for Archipelago 0.6.7 compatibility, and I've repaired the errors involved with Chaos Chips and Progressive Wink.

entry.title: Astalon v0.99.2
entry.content:
- Fix void portal shuffle - Fix using elevator when you only have 1 destination unlocked - Fix displayed item names when walking into a key door - Bump mc.net - Disable entrance warp when using starting location rando
**More Information**: https://github.com/drtchops/Archipelago/blob/astalon/worlds/astalon/docs/en_Astalon.md **Setup Guide**: https://github.com/drtchops/Archipelago/blob/astalon/worlds/astalon/docs/setup_en.md **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/drtchops/Archipelago-Astalon/compare/v1.0.0-rc1...v1.0.0-rc2

entry.title: Refunct v1.0.1
entry.content: Two small fixes, no need to update your yaml. - Re-added the Seeker Minigame completion message. - Fixed Seeker UT bug (it now works correctly with Universal Tracker)

entry.title: Total War Warhammer 3 v0.9.2
entry.content:
REQUIRES 0.6.7 BUILD OF ARCHIPELAGO
Pre-release updates do not change spheres gamemode.
Added Universal Tracker integration. Added BattleSanity option [Experimental]. Added DespoilerSanity option [Experimental]. Added missing lizardmen buildings.
Exceeding admin capacity now provides increasing debuffs to the economy rather than an instant crippling debuff. Traps/Filler that trade settlements now move the camera to the new settlement so the player knows it's happened.
Fixed progressive buildings locking the main settlement building.
Installation instructions can be found [here](https://github.com/jordansds/Archipelago_TWW3_Alt/blob/main/README.md). [FAQ](https://github.com/jordansds/Archipelago_TWW3_Alt/blob/main/FAQ.md).
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jordansds/Archipelago_TWW3_Alt/compare/V0.9.0...v0.9.1

entry.title: Total War Warhammer 3 v0.9.3
entry.content:
Fixed crash when playing as Snikch with ritualshuffle
Potentially updated spheres gamemode to work (It's gone 1am, I haven't tested this yet lol)

entry.content:
## FFX Apworld v0.7.1
### Bug Fixes - Encounter weighting is no longer always active, causing incorrect encounter rates. (@EvelynTSMG)

+ all Location behind Frey missing a movement item fixed + tevi skin fix (return from invis)
### Poptracker + logic with elements fixed

entry.content: - Fixed a logic issue with leaving d7 in winter - Fixed a logic issue with bomb logically counting for 10 times the amount you actually had

entry.title: The Legend of Zelda - Oracle of Seasons v20.1.4
entry.content: Fixed the fix to the logic issue between d7 and the beach of the previous update

entry.title: Garfield Kart - Furious Racing v0.5.1b0
entry.content:
This is an apworld only update.
- Fixed issues with generating when selecting puzzle piece hunt as your goal. - Fixed invalid manifest.json

entry.title: Garfield Kart - Furious Racing v0.5.2b0
entry.content:
**This is an apworld only update.**
- Fixed some restrictive start issues by force disabling 2 option combos. - Removed a redundant item rule

entry.title: Sonic Adventure DX v1.2.1rc0.post0
entry.content:
## [v1.2.1 pre-release 4] - 2026-04-06
### Changed:
- Added springs to reach the Captain Room door from the Egg Carrier Deck. - These connections are now in logic for all characters in every logic level. - Adjusted the positions of the two Private Room entrances on the Egg Carrier Deck map for clarity.
### Fixed:
- Fixed logic issues when starting with the Egg Carrier untransformed.

entry.title: Elder Scrolls Online v0.3.0
entry.content:
This release aims to provide a few features to make the play experience simpler and more streamlined ## Whats new
- New APESO menu which can currently only be toggled by running the command /showapmenu. The AP menu currently only consists of an options page but future UI features and options will be placed inside this menu. - Added toggle in Options Menu for debug mode - added toggle in Options menu for auto running reloadUI when a location is checked
- The mod will now automatically run reloadUI when you check a location. This will not happen if you are in combat. If that happens text will appear in chat telling you it could not run reloadUI and you will have to manually run it. If you get an item sent to you that you want immediately, you will still have to run reloadUI manually. If you do not want this feature enabled you can disable it in the previously mentioned AP menu.
## Misc - Updated API Version to 101049 - Changed /eso_goal client command to /eso_info to better reflect its function

entry.title: Borderlands 2 v67
entry.content:
Want to test on the frontlines? Get the latest developments here. Higher likelihood of showstopping issues. Good luck out there, Slab!
setup instructions on the home page and [readme](https://github.com/EdricY/Bouncy-Loot-God/blob/main/README.md)
[sample yamls](https://github.com/EdricY/Bouncy-Loot-God/tree/main/sample-yamls)

entry.title: Pokemon Stadium v0.5.4
entry.content: VictoryCondition option should work now. Introduced a fix for the ValueError bug seen in the client sometimes.

entry.title: Casualties: Unknown v0.8.0.post2
entry.content:
### Casualties: Unknown Archipelago v0.8.0 This update is NOT compatible with worlds generated on previous versions!
For instructions on how to set up and play this mod, check the [README](https://github.com/Nichologeam/CUAP/blob/master/README.md).
# Additions - Custom Translation Support - This is currently only half supported. Text that dynamically changes at runtime and all error messages are translateable. Hardcoded lines of text are not translateable, but will be eventually. - To create a custom translation, make a copy of the EN.json text file inside the new `lang` folder. Make sure that text inside \<angled brackets\> is left untouched, else things can break. - To load custom translations, use the newly created `aplang` command *on the main menu*. Using it ingame will ***reset your current run***! - Craftsanity (#38) [APWorld Change] - Crafting each recipe in the game sends a check. Should be paired with Randomize Recipes and Skillsanity - Minigame Randomization [APWorld Change] - Start without minigames, and receive them through Archipelago. YAML option for one item for each minigame, or one item for all minigames - Suicide and Self Harm are unrandomized, as I don't want to make sensitive players uncomfortable with an item name referencing suicide # Changes - Update to Archipelago 0.6.7 [APWorld Change] - Archipelago Item names are now generic, though the descriptions are still the same - Skillsanity has been moved to the Recipes option group [APWorld Change] - The Experiment dialog system has been removed entirely. - It was heavily outdated, influenced by basegame mechanics, and would have required a complete rewrite for language support. It may return eventually, but do not count on it. # Fixes - Archipelago Items in Survivor inventories will no longer inherit the name of the last Archipelago Item - Fixed "Material pouch" being named "Material bag" [APWorld Change] - Fixed the Moodlesanity Questboard reroll cooldown not showing for the first second - Fixed the mood increase sound being played when receiving "Despair" - Fixed the main menu being a black screen after disconnecting under rare conditions # Known Issues - Getting two traps in quick succession can cause the incorrect slot name inside the trap Moodle - Universal Tracker shows the wrong Moodles being in logic when Moodlesanity is set to Questboard - A NullReferenceException is sometimes thrown when the Layer Selector opens. This seems to be an issue with how Harmony interacts with Prefix patches on IEnumerators - If more than one Archipelago Item exists, they will all share the same sprite and description. This is a limitation of the original game, and will not be fixed - Craftsanity checks will still be sent if crafting fails due to low INT. This is a bug in the original game, and will not be fixed
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Nichologeam/CUAP/compare/v0.7.2...v0.8.0

entry.title: Autopelago v0.11.0
entry.content:
# https://autopelago.app
The game client has been completely rewritten from scratch. It runs in the browser now.
The .apworld now has a manifest, but has otherwise not changed since 0.10.3.
# Changes since 0.11.15
1. "Back to Main Menu" button now uses the parameters from the current game instead of the last thing you typed in on any Connect screen. This mostly matters when you have multiple tabs open at once. 2. Fix rendering of objects on the map, mainly quest markers (especially the rotated ones), so they look more correct at more map sizes. 3. Go back to auto-reconnecting after #143 reverted it. It seems likely that the cause of room resurrections was misattributed to Autopelago.
Everything from the original native version *should* be present in this one. If not, then that's a bug. Please report it: the native version will stop working in APWorld versions 1.x and above.
# Other changes since 0.10.4
1. You no longer need to run any native executable files. It's a web app now, so just go to that URL. - If you want to self-host it, it's all static files - this release should have a "dist.zip" asset that you can just drop into any reasonable static web server. - You will **need** to self-host if you want it to work with an Archipelago server that doesn't use HTTPS. 2. You can now launch directly to the game if you know all the magical things to type into the URL fragment. This is mostly provided so you can refresh the page without having to click through the menu again. 3. As is web tradition, most errors that happen when the game is running will not automatically take you out of the game screen. Instead, they will just create banners along the top that you have to click to dismiss. Hopefully, this will never happen to you (of course, it happened to me constantly while developing this). 4. Not only is this a web app (and so it finally runs on Mac and Android), but it's a **progressive** web app. This means that you can "install" it as a native app on Android, even. 5. Interval between "when STILL blocked" messages is now configurable (though it's still got a minimum of 15 minutes so that you don't spam everyone). 6. There's now a separate "Connecting..." screen between the settings page and the main game screen, instead of showing a version of the game screen instantly and then modifying it once you're finally connected. 7. When you get disconnected for any reason (other than clicking the button to go back to the menu), you now get spat back to the same "Connecting..." screen (with a different message) and it will try to reconnect you automatically, at least once every 30 seconds. 8. The game version is now visible in the title bar and on the game screen. This is more relevant here because browsers like to cache way too much stuff. 9. Somewhat redesigned the layout of the game screen to be a bit clearer. Instead of shorthand "RAT" / "NOM" / etc., we have a "RATS: ##" expander, and then all the active buffs / debuffs are visible with their full names under it. This takes up a lot of screen space, so you can collapse it to just show the rat count. 10. The left panel can also shrink MUCH more than before. 11. The position of the grid separator is now saved between runs. 12. If your slot has an alias, we now show that (without the parenthetical slot name) instead of always just showing the official slot name. 13. Chat commands can now be addressed to the rat's alias (with or without the parenthetical slot name) instead of always having to be its slot name, provided that nobody else in the room has picked the same alias. 14. Added a simple chat tab to just send / receive messages in the room, so that you don't HAVE to flip back and forth. It's not quite as fancy as the standard text client, but it should be good enough. 15. Elements that you can interact with all support tab-focusing, and the tooltips as well. 16. When a lot of things happen all at once that the game hasn't animated yet, the app will try to play back everything that happened. This was important when moving to a web app, because browsers don't render things when the tab is in the background or minimized, so when you come back, a LOT of things could have changed. In fact, when LOTS of things changed, it speeds up the playback up to 5x so that you can "catch up" to the current state more quickly. 17. When clicking on an item in the panel to request a hint, the confirmation box now shows up right next to it instead of in the middle of the screen. 18. The "u win." screen now just covers a *part* of the map instead of taking you all the way out. 19. Fix an oversight where the self-host bundle was still expecting to run on `https://autopelago.app` 20. Add a discriminator to the beginning of the title for the benefit of those with multiple tabs 21. #129: Bring back the ability to show the path to the target by clicking on the rat. 22. Fix a minor issue where the rat would face its ultimate target instead of the next location along the route to it. 23. Fix an issue where the rat would go to the nearest user-requested location instead of going in order of when they were requested. 24. (After the failed v0.11.2) probably fix a calculation error in the "show path" calculation? 25. #130: Bring back the ability to personalize your rat. 26. Replace Pixi.js with native elements, which should unblock those getting the error "`CanvasRenderer` is not yet implemented". This also probably brings with it a mixed bag of performance penalties and performance improvements. 27. Fix several ways in which the page could look self-inconsistent while the rat is still catching up to where the server is. 28. Correct the size of the "Personalize Your Rat!" button on the main page for non-Firefox browsers. 29. Remove a brief flicker of the "Personalize Your Rat!" window's header text. It might still sometimes shift, but generally shouldn't have any frames where the text goes outside the window like it did. 30. Add tooltips for the filler squares to show their names, exactly the same as tooltips for landmarks. 31. Add information about known hints onto these tooltips. 32. Move some quest markers out of the way so that they no longer overlap filler squares. 33. Shrink landmark images so that they don't crowd their filler squares. 34. Extra-shrink the hitboxes around the quest markers to make their nearby filler squares easier to get to. 35. Add the ability to "hyper-focus" a location by clicking on it (or pressing Enter on it if you are tabbing through with your keyboard). This "hyper-focus" can only be adjusted by interacting with it in the browser (no chat commands supported, by design) for multiple reasons. 36. #134: Fix an issue where the rat's focused location(s) would get cleared — even if the location wasn't checked — if it happened to land on the location without any actions remaining to make an attempt at checking it. 37. Fix an oversight from 0.11.5 where the animation checkboxes did nothing. - In addition to restoring the original few things that these checkboxes control: 1. Transitions on Food/Energy/Luck bars will be animated (or not) according to the player animation checkbox. 2. Tile examples in the tooltips and the "u win." dialog will be animated (or not) along with the tiles, synchronized to the same frame. 3. The dashed line showing the path from the rat to its target location will be animated (or not) according to the player animation checkbox. 4. The rat token will animate its movement from one location to the next (or not) according to the player animation checkbox. 5. On systems where the user has indicated a [preference for "reduced motion"](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/At-rules/@media/prefers-reduced-motion#user_preferences), the two checkboxes will be unchecked by default. 38. #143: Stop automatically reconnecting after getting disconnected. This has been resurrecting rooms that had previously timed out, which is rude. Some version of this MAY come back in the future, if I can find a way to detect when it would be OK. 39. #142: The dashed path to the rat's current target will no longer get misaligned and shrunk on games that don't go all the way to Snakes on a Planet. 40. #138: The sidebar now has an extra spot at the beginning that shows the number of all rats. This lets you do the same stuff that the other icons in this panel let you do: click to hint for any rat (i.e., any item in the "Rats" item group) and hover over it to see a tooltip with all the hinted rat locations. 41. Because I kept getting annoyed while testing the previous one, tooltips now stay around when the cursor goes inside them instead of needing the cursor to stay inside the original trigger box. This means that you can now highlight text from these tooltips with your mouse and copy-paste. 42. I might have fixed a bug where sometimes the rat wouldn't send its chat messages immediately, but would wait for some arbitrary event to happen in the future. I have no idea why I wrote it the way I did at first... 43. Whenever we see `{RANDOM_PLAYER}` in any message from your YAML file, we'll replace that with the current alias of a random player. I tried to make this as useful and sensible as I could: - If you are the only player (on your team), it will always just pick you. Otherwise, it will never pick you, but instead: - If any other players (on your team) are connected and haven't completed their goal yet, then it will pick a random one of them. Otherwise, it will pick a random other player (on your team). - If there are multiple `{RANDOM_PLAYER}` placeholders in a single message, then we'll try to pick as many **different** players as possible (in random order still), only repeating a player if there are more placeholders than the number of players allowed by the above rules. To be precise, we sample randomly without replacement from the list until the list is empty, then we refill it and repeat the process until all placeholders have been filled. 44. Fix an oversight that caused messages to get repeatedly processed over and over again. This was most visible when telling the rat to do something: it would go into an infinite loop of acknowledging the request forever. 45. Fix an oversight where a horizontal scroll bar could appear under normal conditions just because of how close certain locations are to the edge. 46. Set the base URL to allow self-hosting it somewhere other than the site root (e.g., if I were to make subpaths like `https://old.autopelago.app/0.11.13` to run that older version). 47. Start looking for the bits that version 1.0.0 of the APWorld will (later!) start to send us to support more flavor items with only Archipelago-side updates. 48. For games with passwords, start encrypting what shows up in the URL bar. There's a lot more to it than that sounds: the encrypted password uses `&W=EncryptedPassword`, but you can still manually plug in `&w=OriginalPassword`. The encryption is there so that you can reload / bookmark the page and still connect to the game just fine, as long as you do so within the same browser profile (i.e., you can't paste your URL from Firefox to Chrome or to someone else's computer, the key is only stored locally). 49. Fix an unnecessary error when connecting to multiworlds generated from APWorld files predating April 1, 2025. Ongoing support for multiworlds generated from old 0.x APWorld files is not guaranteed, but if the only thing I need to do is slightly tweak a version number check, then that's no big deal.

entry.title: Elder Scrolls Online v0.3.0
entry.content:
This update has no updates to APWorld code. if you are running the apworld from PR6 then you do not need to update This update is compatible with games generated on previous PR versions ## Whats new - Added warning if character alliance does not match the alliance selected at generation
## Bug Fixes - fixed placement of close button on AP Menu - Fixed bug with the zone warning popup code

entry.title: Void Stranger v0.9.2.1
entry.content: ## What's Changed Fixed a logic error in the rule for reaching the "Mural of Pride" location.

entry.title: Bloons TD6 v0.5.1
entry.content: - Added option to set maximum Medals

entry.title: Autopelago v0.11.0
entry.content:
# https://autopelago.app
The game client has been completely rewritten from scratch. It runs in the browser now.
The .apworld now has a manifest, but has otherwise not changed since 0.10.3.
# Changes since 0.11.15
1. "Back to Main Menu" button now uses the parameters from the current game instead of the last thing you typed in on any Connect screen. This mostly matters when you have multiple tabs open at once. 2. Fix rendering of objects on the map, mainly quest markers (especially the rotated ones), so they look more correct at more map sizes. 3. Go back to auto-reconnecting after #143 reverted it. It seems likely that the cause of room resurrections was misattributed to Autopelago. 4. Fix an oversight from 0.11.16 that caused filler squares to stay yellow even after being checked.
Everything from the original native version *should* be present in this one. If not, then that's a bug. Please report it: the native version will stop working in APWorld versions 1.x and above.
# Other changes since 0.10.4
1. You no longer need to run any native executable files. It's a web app now, so just go to that URL. - If you want to self-host it, it's all static files - this release should have a "dist.zip" asset that you can just drop into any reasonable static web server. - You will **need** to self-host if you want it to work with an Archipelago server that doesn't use HTTPS. 2. You can now launch directly to the game if you know all the magical things to type into the URL fragment. This is mostly provided so you can refresh the page without having to click through the menu again. 3. As is web tradition, most errors that happen when the game is running will not automatically take you out of the game screen. Instead, they will just create banners along the top that you have to click to dismiss. Hopefully, this will never happen to you (of course, it happened to me constantly while developing this). 4. Not only is this a web app (and so it finally runs on Mac and Android), but it's a **progressive** web app. This means that you can "install" it as a native app on Android, even. 5. Interval between "when STILL blocked" messages is now configurable (though it's still got a minimum of 15 minutes so that you don't spam everyone). 6. There's now a separate "Connecting..." screen between the settings page and the main game screen, instead of showing a version of the game screen instantly and then modifying it once you're finally connected. 7. When you get disconnected for any reason (other than clicking the button to go back to the menu), you now get spat back to the same "Connecting..." screen (with a different message) and it will try to reconnect you automatically, at least once every 30 seconds. 8. The game version is now visible in the title bar and on the game screen. This is more relevant here because browsers like to cache way too much stuff. 9. Somewhat redesigned the layout of the game screen to be a bit clearer. Instead of shorthand "RAT" / "NOM" / etc., we have a "RATS: ##" expander, and then all the active buffs / debuffs are visible with their full names under it. This takes up a lot of screen space, so you can collapse it to just show the rat count. 10. The left panel can also shrink MUCH more than before. 11. The position of the grid separator is now saved between runs. 12. If your slot has an alias, we now show that (without the parenthetical slot name) instead of always just showing the official slot name. 13. Chat commands can now be addressed to the rat's alias (with or without the parenthetical slot name) instead of always having to be its slot name, provided that nobody else in the room has picked the same alias. 14. Added a simple chat tab to just send / receive messages in the room, so that you don't HAVE to flip back and forth. It's not quite as fancy as the standard text client, but it should be good enough. 15. Elements that you can interact with all support tab-focusing, and the tooltips as well. 16. When a lot of things happen all at once that the game hasn't animated yet, the app will try to play back everything that happened. This was important when moving to a web app, because browsers don't render things when the tab is in the background or minimized, so when you come back, a LOT of things could have changed. In fact, when LOTS of things changed, it speeds up the playback up to 5x so that you can "catch up" to the current state more quickly. 17. When clicking on an item in the panel to request a hint, the confirmation box now shows up right next to it instead of in the middle of the screen. 18. The "u win." screen now just covers a *part* of the map instead of taking you all the way out. 19. Fix an oversight where the self-host bundle was still expecting to run on `https://autopelago.app` 20. Add a discriminator to the beginning of the title for the benefit of those with multiple tabs 21. #129: Bring back the ability to show the path to the target by clicking on the rat. 22. Fix a minor issue where the rat would face its ultimate target instead of the next location along the route to it. 23. Fix an issue where the rat would go to the nearest user-requested location instead of going in order of when they were requested. 24. (After the failed v0.11.2) probably fix a calculation error in the "show path" calculation? 25. #130: Bring back the ability to personalize your rat. 26. Replace Pixi.js with native elements, which should unblock those getting the error "`CanvasRenderer` is not yet implemented". This also probably brings with it a mixed bag of performance penalties and performance improvements. 27. Fix several ways in which the page could look self-inconsistent while the rat is still catching up to where the server is. 28. Correct the size of the "Personalize Your Rat!" button on the main page for non-Firefox browsers. 29. Remove a brief flicker of the "Personalize Your Rat!" window's header text. It might still sometimes shift, but generally shouldn't have any frames where the text goes outside the window like it did. 30. Add tooltips for the filler squares to show their names, exactly the same as tooltips for landmarks. 31. Add information about known hints onto these tooltips. 32. Move some quest markers out of the way so that they no longer overlap filler squares. 33. Shrink landmark images so that they don't crowd their filler squares. 34. Extra-shrink the hitboxes around the quest markers to make their nearby filler squares easier to get to. 35. Add the ability to "hyper-focus" a location by clicking on it (or pressing Enter on it if you are tabbing through with your keyboard). This "hyper-focus" can only be adjusted by interacting with it in the browser (no chat commands supported, by design) for multiple reasons. 36. #134: Fix an issue where the rat's focused location(s) would get cleared — even if the location wasn't checked — if it happened to land on the location without any actions remaining to make an attempt at checking it. 37. Fix an oversight from 0.11.5 where the animation checkboxes did nothing. - In addition to restoring the original few things that these checkboxes control: 1. Transitions on Food/Energy/Luck bars will be animated (or not) according to the player animation checkbox. 2. Tile examples in the tooltips and the "u win." dialog will be animated (or not) along with the tiles, synchronized to the same frame. 3. The dashed line showing the path from the rat to its target location will be animated (or not) according to the player animation checkbox. 4. The rat token will animate its movement from one location to the next (or not) according to the player animation checkbox. 5. On systems where the user has indicated a [preference for "reduced motion"](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/At-rules/@media/prefers-reduced-motion#user_preferences), the two checkboxes will be unchecked by default. 38. #143: Stop automatically reconnecting after getting disconnected. This has been resurrecting rooms that had previously timed out, which is rude. Some version of this MAY come back in the future, if I can find a way to detect when it would be OK. 39. #142: The dashed path to the rat's current target will no longer get misaligned and shrunk on games that don't go all the way to Snakes on a Planet. 40. #138: The sidebar now has an extra spot at the beginning that shows the number of all rats. This lets you do the same stuff that the other icons in this panel let you do: click to hint for any rat (i.e., any item in the "Rats" item group) and hover over it to see a tooltip with all the hinted rat locations. 41. Because I kept getting annoyed while testing the previous one, tooltips now stay around when the cursor goes inside them instead of needing the cursor to stay inside the original trigger box. This means that you can now highlight text from these tooltips with your mouse and copy-paste. 42. I might have fixed a bug where sometimes the rat wouldn't send its chat messages immediately, but would wait for some arbitrary event to happen in the future. I have no idea why I wrote it the way I did at first... 43. Whenever we see `{RANDOM_PLAYER}` in any message from your YAML file, we'll replace that with the current alias of a random player. I tried to make this as useful and sensible as I could: - If you are the only player (on your team), it will always just pick you. Otherwise, it will never pick you, but instead: - If any other players (on your team) are connected and haven't completed their goal yet, then it will pick a random one of them. Otherwise, it will pick a random other player (on your team). - If there are multiple `{RANDOM_PLAYER}` placeholders in a single message, then we'll try to pick as many **different** players as possible (in random order still), only repeating a player if there are more placeholders than the number of players allowed by the above rules. To be precise, we sample randomly without replacement from the list until the list is empty, then we refill it and repeat the process until all placeholders have been filled. 44. Fix an oversight that caused messages to get repeatedly processed over and over again. This was most visible when telling the rat to do something: it would go into an infinite loop of acknowledging the request forever. 45. Fix an oversight where a horizontal scroll bar could appear under normal conditions just because of how close certain locations are to the edge. 46. Set the base URL to allow self-hosting it somewhere other than the site root (e.g., if I were to make subpaths like `https://old.autopelago.app/0.11.13` to run that older version). 47. Start looking for the bits that version 1.0.0 of the APWorld will (later!) start to send us to support more flavor items with only Archipelago-side updates. 48. For games with passwords, start encrypting what shows up in the URL bar. There's a lot more to it than that sounds: the encrypted password uses `&W=EncryptedPassword`, but you can still manually plug in `&w=OriginalPassword`. The encryption is there so that you can reload / bookmark the page and still connect to the game just fine, as long as you do so within the same browser profile (i.e., you can't paste your URL from Firefox to Chrome or to someone else's computer, the key is only stored locally). 49. Fix an unnecessary error when connecting to multiworlds generated from APWorld files predating April 1, 2025. Ongoing support for multiworlds generated from old 0.x APWorld files is not guaranteed, but if the only thing I need to do is slightly tweak a version number check, then that's no big deal.

entry.title: Crash2 v0.3.0rc2.post1
entry.content:
Note: still pre release because warp rando could still be broken (and I don't want to test anymore rn)
## Additions: - Updated some option descriptions to be more detailed and added option groups - New Option to fill wumpa checks locally - This is to prevent other worlds from getting clogged with Crash 2 filler when playing fruitsanity - New Option to exclude difficult wumpa checks - This will prevent wumpas in difficult/annoying spots from containing useful items (affected locations have a "*" in their name) - Added the two wumpas from Cold Hard Crash that I previously thought were unobtainable as actual checks - Warp randomizer now has Universal Tracker support
## Bug Fixes: - Letting the demo run will no longer collect wumpa fruit checks - A gameover during a no lives trap will no longer give extra lives - Made Small Crash a bit bigger (default scale mult changed from 20% -> 33%), to try preventing Crash from often getting stuck in the floor when crouching/sliding - This was the smallest I could make Crash while not getting stuck on the grated floor in Hangin' Out - Small Crash trap will now be disabled for levels that have Polar (the white bear) - Shrinking Crash's scale had the effect of limiting how far from the center of the path you could go, making it quite unplayable - Small Crash now moves Crash up when activating, so that you won't fall while hanging - Fixed typo in trap_duration's display name
## Issues: - Warp randomizer is not fixed

entry.title: The Legend of Zelda - Oracle of Seasons v20.1.5
entry.content: ## 🛠️ Bugfixes - Fixed the natzu bridge - Added more space for text used to display other people's items

entry.title: The Legend of Zelda - Oracle of Seasons v20.1.6
entry.content: Fixed warp to start which was set in natzu for debug purposes

entry.title: Pokemon Crystal v5.4.2
entry.content:
# Changelog
Since [5.4.1](https://github.com/gerbiljames/Archipelago-Crystal/releases/tag/5.4.1)
## Bug Fixes - The Options Creator once again accepts `_All` and `_Random` for options which support it. - Fix a possible game crash when viewing the MORE Pokedex page for a species found on Routes 12, 13 and 32 fishing. - Pokemon revived after being fainted from an Explosion Trap in the overworld will no longer keep any status they had. - Fix running from the Pokecenter 2F battle not always succeeding. - Fix Bug Catching Contest encounters not respecting the more uncaught encounters setting. - `randomize_moves` now accepts `_All` and `_Random`.
**This release uses a new patching system which can apply ROM-side fixes to an already generated seed. Simply re-patch your ROM to do this.**
_This release is backwards compatible with seeds generated on 5.4.0 or later._

entry.title: The Legend of Zelda - Oracle of Seasons v20.1.6
entry.content: Removed debugging code causing issues and creating an unneeded file

entry.title: Donkey Kong 64 v1.5.2
entry.content:
DK64 Randomizer APWorld file for Archipelago version 1.5.2
This release contains the dk64.apworld file that can be used with the Archipelago multiworld randomizer.

entry.title: Void Stranger v0.9.2.2
entry.content:
## What's Changed for v0.9.2.2
## What's Changed * Merge pull request #1 from VirulenceDev/workflow-release-draft by @VirulenceDev in https://github.com/CriminalPancake/void-stranger-ap/pull/18 * Add itemgroup aliases by @Rooby-Roo in https://github.com/CriminalPancake/void-stranger-ap/pull/14 * remove unused imports by @Rooby-Roo in https://github.com/CriminalPancake/void-stranger-ap/pull/15
## New Contributors * @VirulenceDev made their first contribution in https://github.com/CriminalPancake/void-stranger-ap/pull/18 * @Rooby-Roo made their first contribution in https://github.com/CriminalPancake/void-stranger-ap/pull/14
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/CriminalPancake/void-stranger-ap/compare/v0.9.2.1...v0.9.2.2
